shootDirection: a long-lens candid across a busy street, the group facing the camera compressed against shopfronts, strangers blurring past in the foreground
concept: Busy high street telephoto candid against vivid shopfronts
scene: Shot from across a busy shopping street just before sunset with a long lens, the four selected figures are compressed tightly against a run of saturated storefronts and glossy blue metal shutters, every face large and tack-sharp while out-of-focus commuters streak through the foreground as smears of color and motion. At far left, the pale blonde woman from 1 is half-leaning back into a shop window, black top and vivid lipstick catching a warm neon reflection; beside her, the long-haired moustached figure from 2 stands slightly forward in loose black, hands open at waist height as if mid-conversation. Near the center-right, the woman from 7 in her dark bob and speckled jacket turns three-quarters toward the others with one shoulder tucked to the shutter, while at the far right the bald man from 9, still in his dramatic studded black coat and dark glasses, anchors the frame with folded arms and a cool, unreadable stare. The light is luminous and punchy, mixing low golden sun with electric shop signage so skin glows and blacks stay rich; their styling feels intentionally clashing yet cohesive—psychedelic pattern, severe black minimalism, sharp new-wave tailoring, and full metal grandeur—captured as a real caught moment rather than a lineup, with staggered posture, slight overlap, and different eyelines giving the group a charged street-level chemistry.
prompt: Create one original, photorealistic 16:9 group photograph that brings together the musicians shown across the 4 reference images into a single shared real-world scene. Scene: Shot from across a busy shopping street just before sunset with a long lens, the four selected figures are compressed tightly against a run of saturated storefronts and glossy blue metal shutters, every face large and tack-sharp while out-of-focus commuters streak through the foreground as smears of color and motion. At far left, the pale blonde woman from 1 is half-leaning back into a shop window, black top and vivid lipstick catching a warm neon reflection; beside her, the long-haired moustached figure from 2 stands slightly forward in loose black, hands open at waist height as if mid-conversation. Near the center-right, the woman from 7 in her dark bob and speckled jacket turns three-quarters toward the others with one shoulder tucked to the shutter, while at the far right the bald man from 9, still in his dramatic studded black coat and dark glasses, anchors the frame with folded arms and a cool, unreadable stare. The light is luminous and punchy, mixing low golden sun with electric shop signage so skin glows and blacks stay rich; their styling feels intentionally clashing yet cohesive—psychedelic pattern, severe black minimalism, sharp new-wave tailoring, and full metal grandeur—captured as a real caught moment rather than a lineup, with staggered posture, slight overlap, and different eyelines giving the group a charged street-level chemistry. Keep every person clearly recognisable and faithful to their reference photo - preserve each face, skin tone, hair, facial hair, glasses, and overall likeness exactly as shown. Do not invent anyone who is not in the reference photos. Keep this an intimate, well-composed group, not a crowd. Where a reference photo shows a band, feature only its single most recognisable member rather than every member. Frame the group tightly - close or medium-close - so every face is large in the frame, sharp, and richly detailed, never small or distant. Arrange them naturally within the scene as one connected group sharing the same moment and light, like a real posed or candid group photo. Vary posture, depth, and where people are looking - this is a caught moment, not a band-publicity lineup evenly spaced and facing the camera. Not separate cut-outs floating side by side, and never a grid, row, or panel of squares. Render photorealistically: real skin texture, true-to-life lighting and depth of field, captured as if shot on a professional camera. This is a real photograph, not an illustration, drawing, painting, cartoon, or render. Colour treatment: bright, vivid, richly saturated editorial colour with luminous highlights and punchy contrast, drawing on electric blue, lipstick red, golden amber, jet black, violet. Mood: cool, charged, urban. Do not add any extra people beyond the reference subjects. Do not duplicate, merge, blend, or distort faces. Do not include any text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, or signage anywhere in the image. Avoid: readable text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, signage, grid layout, contact sheet, evenly tiled squares, raw album-cover thumbnails, collage of separate panels, illustration, illustrated, drawing, painting, cartoon, comic, anime, vector art, sketch, flat graphic style, extra people, background crowd, duplicated faces, cloned faces, merged faces, blended faces, morphed features, distorted faces, deformed hands, extra limbs. Photorealistic, sharp, high quality, flattering, with real depth and a strong sense of place.
